title: Tonight the Music Seems So Loud by Sathnam Sanghera review – a heartbreaking portrait of George Michael
description:
This affecting exploration of the troubled genius’s impact is packed with anecdote, sharp analysis and social context

In 1998, George Michael was arrested for public lewdness in an LA lavatory, an incident that finally led the singer to publicly come out. The following day, Sathnam Sanghera found himself unable to leave his room at university: the doorway had been mockingly plastered with tabloid newspaper headlines – “ZIP ME UP BEFORE YOU GO-GO!” – by fellow students aware of his longs
hook: George Michael's arrest forced him out of the closet. Sanghera asks what it cost him—and why we needed that rupture to see him whole.

title: Shakespeare and Reality Televison Really Aren’t That Different
description: When the first massively popular reality TV shows hit the air in the early 2000s, I remember thinking in my naïveté: Surely this is just a passing fad. I was entering my teens and, like so many closeted queer youth
hook: Reality TV isn't lowbrow spectacle—it's Shakespeare for the streaming age, complete with tragic arcs and moral chaos.

title: The Sound of Imminence: Ruth Ozeki in Praise of the Typewriter
description: Spring is the time of year I buy typewriters. It must be the change of season—green shoots pushing up through the thawing earth, bulbs budding and blooming, trees leafing out—which has nothing to do with typewriters, but everything to do
hook: Ruth Ozeki buys typewriters every spring. A poet's meditation on why the mechanical matters more than ever.

title: That’s Enough, Euphoria
description: In its final episode, the series gave in to its worst impulses while trying to sell a poignant message.
hook: Euphoria's finale abandoned everything that made it hurt and settled for sentimentality instead—a series betraying its own brilliance.

title: Can A.I. Produce Writing That We Actually Want to Read?
description: I recently created a simple test, which convinced me that the answer is no.
hook: AI can generate correct sentences forever. What it cannot do is make you feel anything. Here's the test that proved it.
